meta.role: Program Manager, uBecome Intern Experience & Community

header.location: Portland, OR (Open to Remote or Relocation)

summary:
I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Talent Development & Program Manager who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, developing programs grounded in insights, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact.
Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve talent experience—through human-centered, data-driven approaches.

bullets.text: Establish onboarding processes, enablement resources, and training materials for 70+ cross-functional staff, reducing onboarding time by 75%
bullets.original: Develop guides and workflows for 70+ cross-functional staff, improving program knowledge and reducing onboarding time by 75%
bullets.reason: JD: 'develop onboarding resources' + 'cross-functional partnership with People Ops' — direct match to onboarding documentation at scale

bullets.text: Facilitate listening sessions to analyze needs of 25+ global stakeholders, resulting in 3 new learning programs aligned with student needs
bullets.original: Conduct needs assessments with 25+ global stakeholders to identify learning gaps and design 3 new educational programs
bullets.reason: JD: 'designing scalable programming' for early talent — InGenius's student population maps directly to intern/early-career; 'listening sessions' surfaces the discovery muscle

bullets.text: Manage 20+ programs and 10+ vendors, refining program positioning and communications strategy via stakeholder feedback analysis
bullets.reason: JD: 'partner with Brand, Communications, People Ops' + 'employer branding via content' — communications strategy + program positioning is exactly that intersection

bullets.text: Facilitate training webinars for 600+ participants, increasing program understanding and driving a 78% conversion rate
bullets.original: Facilitate training webinars for 600+ participants, boosting program engagement and achieving a 78% program enrollment rate
bullets.reason: JD: 'create immersive learning experiences' — direct phrase pickup; enrollment metric stays as quant proof

bullets.text: Analyzed 2,000+ employee experiences to amplify employee voice and guide C-suite decisions on {data|satisfaction drivers}
bullets.reason: JD: 'data-driven approach to identifying insights' — leads NextGen with the 2,000+ data-analysis bullet for stronger first impression

bullets.text: Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming {data|feedback data} into actionable reports that informed {ex|engagement strategies}
Provided consultations to improve strategic plans and internal training on {prog|DEI, Inclusive Leadership}, and performance review
bullets.reason: JD: 'inclusive engagement program design' — DEI + Inclusive Leadership consultation directly maps

bullets.text: Managed weekly {tools|SharePoint dashboards} for {data|people metrics}, Net Promoter Score, and survey insights to drive {ex|employee recognition}
bullets.original: Improved employee experience via 5 cross-functional projects focusing on survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D
bullets.reason: JD: 'data-driven approach to identifying insights' + 'employer branding via content' — surfaces a concrete dashboarding tool, NPS metric, and recognition program work that drops the generic 'cross-functional projects' summary bullet

bullets.text: Initiated a DEI pilot with an {prog|Inclusive Leadership workshop} for 12 senior leaders, later expanded to 23 other global locations
bullets.reason: JD: 'inclusive engagement program design' + 'scalable programming' — pilot scaled to 23 locations is the textbook proof

bullets.text: Optimized {coord|onboarding processes} across 3 global locations by conducting process analysis and clarifying job responsibilities
bullets.reason: JD: 'develop onboarding resources' — surfaces global onboarding work earlier in the bullet stack

bullets.text: Facilitated smooth organizational change by directing a Mergers & Acquisitions project, overseeing {cross|cross-functional stakeholders}
Strengthened engagement via a People newsletter and HR Asia award-winning well-being initiatives, recognized among 1,000+ competitors
bullets.reason: JD: 'strengthen employer branding through intern-led content' — newsletter + award-winning comms maps to employer branding via content

bullets.text: Organized {prog|campus recruiting events} and moderated panels with C-suite at top 3 universities, reaching 230+ students and 80+ applicants
bullets.reason: JD: 'university recruiting / early talent' — Kronos's top-3-university campus recruiting is the single strongest pillar bullet for this JD; moved to top of section

bullets.text: Engaged {prog|early-career talent} via personalized communication and customer service, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
bullets.original: Engaged global talent via personalized communication and customer service, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
bullets.reason: JD: 'early talent' — 'global talent' reframed as 'early-career talent' to mirror JD vocabulary

bullets.text: Filled a challenging international role by drafting job descriptions, posting openings, and researching global compensation details
Developed proficiency across various HR functions, ranging from recruitment and compensation to performance management

bullets: Relevant Coursework: Organizational Psychology, Research Methods, Group Dynamics & Leadership, Consumer Psychology

education.degree: BA in Psychology and Education Studies (GPA: 4.00)
bullets: Relevant Coursework: Educational Psychology, Practicum in Education Studies, Psychological Testing, Learning and Motivation

projects: Pro Bono Consulting (Aug 2025–Present): Partner with Organization Development Network (ODN) Oregon on two projects: leading an HR data audit that surfaced $362K in policy-redesign insights, and advising executive leadership on cross-functional role clarity for a 2-year strategic plan
Grad Consulting: Informed Elanco Animal Health employer branding & talent attraction strategy via survey design and focus group execution
Master's Capstone: Examined non-monetary factors shaping employer attractiveness for YouTube via mixed-method research
Scholarships: Freeman Asian Scholarship (4-year full-tuition), Tuck Bridge Scholarship (full)
skills: Technical Skills: Project Management (Asana, Airtable) · Data Analysis (Google Sheets, Excel, SPSS) · ATS (Greenhouse) · HRIS (Rippling, ADP) · Knowledge Management (Notion, SharePoint) · Microsoft Office · Design Tools (Canva, Adobe InDesign) · AI Tools (ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude)
Certifications: HRCI Human Resource Associate Certificate (in progress)
